title: porrum strictum
intro: Porrum strictum, also known as wild garlic or ramsons, is a perennial plant in the lily family that grows in damp, woodland areas. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high sulfur content and active compounds such as alliin and allicin. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce the severity of colds and flu, as well as alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and digestive issues. Porrum strictum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used fresh in cooking to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
